title: Rosa chinensis manettii
taxonomy: Rosa chinensis manettii is a cultivar of the species Rosa chinensis, which is a type of rose native to China. The full classification of this plant includes: genus Rosa, species R. chinensis, and cultivar R. chinensis manettii. Rosa chinensis is a member of the family Rosaceae and the order Rosales. Within the genus Rosa, R. chinensis manettii is classified as a climbing rose. This classification indicates its botanical origins and relationship to other rose species.
uses: Rosa chinensis manettii, also known as the Chinese rose, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant contains antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Rosa chinensis manettii is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses for Rosa chinensis manettii.
benefits: Rosa chinensis manettii is known for its potential health benefits, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. It's said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe and protect the body from damage. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of certain infections. Additionally, Rosa chinensis manettii may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation due to its adaptogenic properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.

constituents: Rosa chinensis manettii contains several active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. parts: Rosa chinensis manettii is a species of rose known for its medicinal properties. The roots and flowers of the plant are often used in traditional medicine, particularly in Chinese and Korean remedies. The roots are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. side_effects: Rosa chinensis manettii, also known as the Chinese rose, contains fragrances and essential oils that may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, or respiratory problems in some individuals. intro: Rosa chinensis manettii, also known as the Chinese rose, is a species of rose plant that is native to China and is widely cultivated for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The health benefits of Rosa chinensis manettii include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Rosa chinensis manettii is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications to promote wound healing and skin health.
